CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Description:

Marsheda is an attorney who is looking to establish a private practice focused on real estate and business law.


Analysis

Marsheda has a current business EwulomiWorks, that she uses for business strategy and public strategy. She wants to expand that business to include authoring books and teaching seminars on alignment theory. In addition, she wants to open a private practice law firm, focused on helping small businesses with complex real estate and legal issues.


Recommendations

Because Marsheda was pulled between the two businesses we focused primarily on EwulomiWorks and her potential pivot. We built a Lean Canvas and started the work to hone in on her value proposition, her target customers, and her differentiators. In addition, we discussed timeline and potential roll out of the legal firm.


Next Steps

Send Marsheda the lean canvas so that she can begin fleshing out her business unique va

Extensive consulting background on the corporate side. Was working for large firm then transitioned to owning her own firm in September, 2024. Is an SCorp and she takes a $65K salary for now. Has $24K in reserves and needs to know more about cash flow and how to grow her business.

More Facts

-has marketing intern Mon-Thurs to work on social media (we'll share social media calendar outline and walk through it in a future session- w/Siera?)

-sells Predictive Index software licenses as part of income stream - this earns her the consulting projects to implement

-exploring an MIT program ($7,000) for AI in Leadership - is it worth the investment?

Goals:

  • Initially determine which services deliver the highest return (keynote speaking for now)
  • Have an LMS in place by July/Aug so that she can get back end subscribers and generate revenue in her sleep through training videos
  • Get a small team in place by year 3 to deliver more basi

Beth is a former banker with a business degree. She seems quite knowledgeable about financing and accounting. She had been baking and selling under cottage food rules for several years and then bought a food trailer that she is now bringing to various events.


We discussed her existing loans and capital structure, ways to consolidate date, move it from personal to business debt, finance growth, etc. We discussed SBA 504 and microloans, and other options with her current lender MSUFCU.


We also discussed outreach, partnering with other aligned businesses, asking her FB followers for suggestions on places she should be, etc. We set a goal that she should net $10k by Labor Day.


We also discussed avoiding burnout, bringing on extra help as needed, evaluating different locations and times, etc.


She does want to have her own commercial kitchen for baking, probably in a new pole barn where she can keep her traile

Rachel is a massage therapist and yoga teacher who rents 2 rooms in a wellness center. She has 1 therapist who contracts with her and she is hoping to find another one soon. She also does occasional workshops in and outside of the studio.


She would like to expand her business, adding an acupuncturist, maybe a chiropractor, an isolation tank, and perhaps a massage school. We discussed all of these in some detail, as well as her operations and marketing (attracting clients has not been a problem for her, she needs more therapists to serve all of the clients on her wait list).


She seems to understand the business well and what she needs to do to grow it. In our next meeting we'll review her plans to purchase her own building and start a school.

The client started his physical therapy out-of-network practice, not to accept insurance in Wyoming, Michigan. The client is renting a 10*10 space in a gym near Burlingham and 28th street.. The client is paying 1000.00 a month for rent, the client has 11 clients to date. ({Prep time, erfc, referral from Malia, LinkedIn, vertical iq, ai)

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

The client did not identify one specific need. I suggested reevaluating not accepting insurance.

RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

Business plan, Flow Chart, order SEO and social media audit, and vertical IQ report, ordered a competitor's list in his zip code.

Initial meeting with Arnaldo. He launched Acqua In Vino in 2015, an artisan grocery store in Wayland. He wants to add a food truck to serve food and host events outdoors during the summer. He needs about $150k for equipment, outdoor renovations and staffing. He applied for the Michigan Main Street program and is waiting to hear back. The plan is to use his $50k cash, get reimbursed with the grant and then reuse it with another $50k borrowed to come up with his total project cost of $150k. We discussed pitch competitions and lending options. He was averse to lending but there isn’t much else out there regarding grants that he hasn’t tapped into yet. We discussed business planning and I sent him the outline to get started on the business plan. I spent some time after the meeting pulling market research and sent via email. He will work on the plan draft and send to me when ready to review.

Emil (EE-mil) has a technical background with experience in security. His product is software that can plug into a system of networked video recorders (NVR) and use AI to monitor activity. He uses primarily open source software and off the shelf hardware, but he has written the user interface and integration. He's interested in protecting his proprietary software, either through copyright, patent, or some other means. He puts stickers on his products with his logo and technical info. Users go to his website to download the software.


He sees this primarily as a B2B product, and his ideal customer already had a network of cameras, but his product will allow low cost, active monitoring. He believes that his customers will be able to install and use his systems without assistance from him, allowing him to sell nationally with little support staff. He intends to have an upfront fee and then ongoing monthly fees.

Christopher is interested in starting a business making electrolytes for lithium ion phosphate batteries, to sell to battery manufacturers in Michigan. He is a nice young man with a few different maintenance and mechanical jobs as work experience, has not worked in the industry, and no experience in chemical science. He is interested in starting this because its a forward industry that is grant supported by the government. He has done considerable online research into the current prices and sources of the ingredients used in this, and found that American manufactured electrolytes are nearly 2000% more expensive than Chinese ones, and has looked into the process of mixing, the sources of raw materials and the end buyer/battery manufacturers.

Jessica has been making and selling 3D printed objects since 2023. She calculates that 80% of her sales come from craft shows, 10% from Etsy and 10% from 3 retailers who have her items on consignment. She did 12 shows in 2023, 36 in 2024 and is planning for 32 this year. Revenues are down this year and she attributes that partially to the overall economy and partially to having more competition form other 3D printers, who she thinks are primarily hobbyists and not serious businesses (so they're willing to take lower margins). She does not have any of her own designs, but licenses from other people.


We discussed improvements to her Etsy store (better photos and descriptions, etc.) and expanding her reach through retailers. We also discussed which shows she should attend and what items she should bring. It seems to me (and, I think to her) that her best opportunities lie in creating unique items without direct competition. This will take her time and mate

ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM


The client is in the early stages of exploring a wholesale food business based on her family’s well-known pretzel recipe. While she has a strong professional background and a passion for baking, she is juggling multiple part-time roles and preparing to transition to full-time teaching in August. She needs support with business structure, packaging, shelf-life considerations, insurance, and how to approach potential wholesale clients. She plans to use a commercial kitchen (rather than operating under Cottage Law), and a lack of experience in food product distribution is a key area that requires further research and guidance.

She works mostly in and around Jackson County, but will travel for a fee to larger jobs. Most of her clients' horses are on a 4-6 week care cycle and she'd like to do 10 horses / day, so that would put her at 200 - 300 horses under her care. She feels that her business is growing steadily and that this growth is pretty typical for a new farrier. However, she would like to speed it up.


She believes that horse ownership is growing in MI, and that most horse owners are very price sensitive. Farriering(?) seems to be a very conservative field, with little technical or operational innovation. It also seems to be a 'commodity service' where people buy on price and convenience. Kady and her husband are retirees who launched their soap making business a few years ago in WA state. They have moved to MI and want to re-establish their business here. They enjoy making and selling soap and have modest profitability expectations.


Much of what we discussed involved taxes. They have a MI CPA and I encouraged them to talk with her about the specifics of their situation and how to best proceed. Problem statement

Overview Mint-to Logic is a lifecycle validation protocol developed and protected under provisional patent by Spencer Southern. It governs identity-bound verification tokens called Mint Units that burn after use. This prevents fraud, double-entry, or unauthorized reuse across systems like digital voting, event ticketing, or cyber access. The protocol works offline and is blockchain-independent. A functional backend prototype has been developed, which logs issuance, validation, and burn events with timestamped integrity. Key Features - Lifecycle enforcement: Verify -> Burn -> Log - Offline-capable and secure - Configurable retry, burn, and Limbo logic - Generates Shepherd Coin metadata upon lifecycle completion - Adaptable to multiple verticals (voting, ticketing, identity access, cybersecurity) - Demonstrated in ValiPass prototype (available for review under NDA) What I'm Seeking Support with grant alignment, funding strategy, or incubator opportuni

Tena has a cottage food based bakery. She sells to friends and family and does farmers markets. She has done a few thousand in sales. She wants to buy a food truck so she can do different kinds of baked goods and sell wholesale. SHe felt this was the least expensive way to get into the market.

Ephy shared she has been doing Accounting for small businesses for years.

Recommendations: I shared the SBDC services and discussed the followi
Introduction call to SBDC services

BRIEF DESCRIPTION OF THE BUSINESS AND/OR OWNERS:

Client is preparing to take over Vera Grace & Etta Mae’s in January 2026. The transition includes seller financing for inventory. The client has management experience but limited retail-specific experience.


AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

While the client brings strong management skills, the lack of retail experience may pose a learning curve, particularly around inventory management, merchandising, and seasonal sales planning. Understanding the store’s peak sales periods and holiday performance is essential for success post-transition.


RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

  • Review historical sales data to monitor peaks and trends, especially around holidays.
  • Analyze percentages of sales by category and timeframe to inform inventory decisions.
  • Provide retail operations template and onboarding

Vanessa is an author. She has written a book called Without Christ, a story of Inspiration that is semi-autobiographical in nature. Her goal is to have the book become a play and a movie.


Vanessa has not done significant marketing to get the book traction.


We discussed, sending her book to notable pastors, who have a mental health platform. I recommended Beth Moore, Priscilla Schreier and Pastor Charlie Dates, as some that I know of. I also recommended that she reach out to local churches and communities, to get the word out about her book. She is also working through Transformation Gems. I also recommended that she think about the lifetime value of a client and how to maximize it by expanding into workbooks, a children's series, and perhaps a devotional.


Vanessa will work on reaching out to different churches and Pastors as well as begin planning the workbook accompaniment for her current short novel.

BRIEF DESCRIPTION OF THE BUSINESS AND/OR OWNERS:

Licensed Michigan Life and Health insurance agent working with Reaum. Sells health insurance, life insurance, Medicare plans, individual and group coverage, as well as ancillary products such as hospital indemnity policies. Currently balancing this business with a full-time HR benefits management job and aims to slowly transition into full-time insurance sales.


AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

  • Unclear on how to expand the business and where to start.

The discussion focused on EK's challenges in managing his catering business alongside his full-time agency job, highlighting the absence of a solid business plan and effective financial tracking. Madison has a vegetable farm and needs some advice on how to figure out how to file their own taxes.


Madison primarily had questions about filing taxes for the farm and what form should be used:


I provided the following info Tax Filing Method for Self-Employed Farmers

Primary Tax Forms:

  • Schedule F (Form 1040) – Used to report farm income and expenses.
  • Schedule SE (Form 1040) – Used to calculate self-employment tax (Social Security and Medicare).
  • Form 1040 – Main tax return form.
  • Form 4562 – For depreciation and amortization of farm equipment, buildings, etc.
  • Form 943 – If you have employees, used for agricultural employment taxes.

Filing Deadlines:

  • Marc

I met with Denise Sutton who was in my Business Plan class at DLP Franklin and she wants to start an assisted living facility. I recommended the following:


  1. Continue to look for full time work in your field to bolster your savings and the amount of money you'll have to use as a down payment on assisted living house - either a purchase or lease.I
  2. Identify the agencies that will refer clients to you, i.e. DHS, Continuance of Care, DHS, Area Concil on Aging, etc.

Sonita is navigating the complex rollout of her on-demand healthcare app, facing hurdles with provider credentialing, insurance compliance, and system debugging—all while preparing for a July launch. Despite having over 100 nurses signed up, she’s contending with delays in background checks and challenges in ensuring providers understand the flexibility of the work. Marketing and user acquisition also present difficulties, particularly in affluent but hard-to-reach areas like Birmingham, and she’s exploring multiple outreach strategies. On top of this, Sonita is balancing the launch of a nonprofit arm, Rush Cares, which will provide free services, and pursuing grant opportunities as a non-dilutive funding strategy.


I recommended Sonita narrow her initial geographic focus to a 15-minute radius around her home to simplify logistics and maximize impact. I also encouraged her to continue testing low-cost marketing approaches and leverage her medical director’s network f

Met with client Kole, who is currently operating a residential window cleaning business in partnership with another individual. The business has generated approximately $11,000 in revenue over the past few weeks. The client is considering hiring employees in the future and is based in Berrien County, Michigan. They are also exploring the possibility of operating the business seasonally in Florida while attending Florida State University.

The client is in the process of formalizing their business structure and asked for guidance on licensing, forming an LLC, and other setup steps. I initially provided the steps out of order, so I clarified the correct sequence during our follow-up:

  1. Choose a business name
  2. Register the LLC through LARA
  3. Apply for an EIN through IRS.gov
  4. Open a business bank account
  5. Contact the Berrien County Clerk to determine local licensing requirements
  6. Obtain business insurance

Reviewed white paper, algorithmic documents for modular autonomous weapons decision system. The DOD submission criteria are in flux, so I identified an alternative possible grant source of the Foresight Institute, which includes a wider eligibility than other sources that are restricted to Academia, NGOs and non profit entities. I advised that a key technical hurdle will be to integrate with code that will be highly proprietary and tightly coupled to the hardware from the manufacturers, which would require a very close partnership with potential collaborators. We discussed an alternative simulation framework that could demonstrate his approach's benefits and possibly open sourcing the testbed to gain visibility and notice.

BRIEF DESCRIPTION OF THE BUSINESS AND/OR OWNERS:

The owner had a recent health scare and liquidated his 401k to pursue his passion for building hotrods. The business has a national client base with customers from places like Wisconsin and Arizona, rather than a strong local presence. Projects are long-term, with some taking up to 2 years.


AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

The business is turning away work due to lack of capital to hire help. The owner performs all work in-house and cannot keep up with demand. While he takes a 60% deposit, the long lead times and scale of work require staffing support to deliver on commitments. The emotional toll of the situation is evident, and there's an urgent need for financial stability and staffing support.


RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

  • Explore short-term working capital solutions or microloans to create a payroll cush

Background: Dusty owns DMT tooling in Warren. He has a cottage on houghton lake. Dusty wants to retire, hand his business to his daughter and start a restaurant in houghton lake. Dusty found a location, developed a concept and is working to purchase the property. The property is listed at $940K. The owner lives in CO. The property has been inspected and appraised at Dusty's expense. Dusty offered $600K and then $650K. The owner didn't counter. During inspection the building authority learned that the building is unsafe; out of code, mold and leaking. The owner has 90 days (now 75) to correct the issues or demolish the building at his cost. If the owner doesn't comply, the township will take the building down and put a lien against the property.


The Growth Team has been advising Dusty since March. Brooks, John and Jim.


Next steps:

I met with Dominique Collins to discuss her mocktail business. She is current;ly working with Motor City Match received a grant for $1500. Per our conversation, I recommend the following>

  2. Continue to work with Kyle at Motor City March on the business plan and send it to me.
  3. Forward a completed copy of your Start-Up Cost Summary for my review.

We meet again later this month.

ClientIntake.BusinessType: Real Estate and Rental and Leasing

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Went over financial forecast with Scott Taylor in detail, comparing against industry standards and variation according to leveraging trade group partners. Eliminated anomalous lumpy entries, smoothing out the revenue growth ramp, and identified key success metrics. Explored sensitivity analysis scenarios and identified key cash flow pinch points. Made a first draft of funding asks, and timing that will accommodate growth plan.

Discussed challenges with trying to advertise through all the noise on social media. Challenged her to come up with hyper-focused marketing message for a local non-online target market of middle aged women wanting to tell their story. Floated idea of women entering workforce in the 1980s.
Vertical IQ research for Electronic component Manufacturers to prepare for Quarterly meeting. Outlook is weak: only .82% annual growth rate for the industry for next 4 years.

ClientIntake.BusinessType: Real Estate and Rental and Leasing

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Thank you for your responses. Please use https://calendly.com/southeastmisbdc/phone-call to set a meeting. 


What to Expect

  • We are funded by the Small Business Administration and our services are at no-cost (except some trainings have a small registration fee). 
  • All information we discuss will remain confidential. This information was in the initial documentation that you completed when you requested consulting.
  • Meetings usually last less than 1 hour. Meetings are only by phone and by appointment.
  • We assist both start-up and existing businesses. Some of the topics that we help with are startup steps, business plans, marketing strategies, financial benchmarking, projections and preparing for bank loans.
  • Michigan SBDC does not have any direct funding for businesses.

I attended a ribbon cutting hosted by the Gratiot Area Chamber at our client's site at 204 E Saginaw St in Breckenridge, MI. The store is accessed from the back lot (it does not face onto Saginaw St) through an entrance from the parking lot. It looks quite nice on the outside and as well on the inside. It is clean and well kept. Angie said that she did a lot of the work herself. The meat cases looked like new and very clean. She explained that she sells her beef that she raises on her property.


During the ribbon cutting, Angie announced that they were awarded a $100K MDARD grant. She also said that it will be parceled out as reimbursements for work completed. Some of it is for training for their store's meat cutters. She may be able to use the funds if she has to move to another location as things are becoming difficult with a dispute with her landlord.

Jonathan,

Thank you so much for your time today. You've done a great job in outlining the vision and strategy for the business. Your accounting background will help you immensely in seeing how to maximize productivity of the properties along with the potential profits.

  • You are currently working a PT job and seeking FT employment - this will be material in the discussions with a lender or investor as you move forward

  • As a matter of reference in positioning your financials, a lender will typically want to see a credit score above 650 (at least 675 - better) to even initiate the conversation

Answered some tax questions and accounting questions by email. Sent tax resources:


Re: wave question


Daniel Yoder <dayoder@mtu.edu>

12:54 PM (1 minute ago)

to Intuitive


Hi Rachel,


Yes, you do need to include all business income and expenses on your tax return. As a single member LLC, your business income and expenses are incorporated into your personal tax return on a Schedule C. If you are using tax preparation software, they usually guide you through the questions. Your business income and expenses are on the 2024 Profit & Loss statement that you pull from Wave, assuming you have everything entered and categorized for the year.


Resources:

https://www.irs.gov/individuals/check-if-you-need-to-file-a-tax-return

https://www.irs.gov/forms-pubs/about-publication-334 - Pages 20-41 talk about business income and expenses

Epoxy Art business and other…


We reviewed his business plan revision. I focused on Market Opportunities – it continued to be clear that Oliver is not prepared to pursue a loan. He was not able to be clear about his customer, his product offering, etc.


I tried to get him to focus one facet and go deeper:

He chose: Custom art, custom epoxy doors and tables.

I chose just doors. I asked him who would by epoxy art doors. He said anybody. I disagreed and asked what type of customer would want an epoxy door? He said anyone looking for a door between $100 and $2,000 dollars. I asked him to be more specific about who would want  $100 epoxy door, said “no one”. Someone interested in an epoxy art door would be spending about $2,000. I asked more questions – it was clear he’s thinking about the work and not the customer.


So I asked why doesn’t he start this on his own? Make a few items and sell them. Learn about his customer and the

Shanna is building Sea Access, a business focused on adaptive sailing through her patented Autonomy Dock, but she’s facing challenges with pricing her consulting services, securing funding in non-coastal regions, protecting her IP in potential partnerships, and balancing operations as a solo founder. She’s also navigating early-stage customer discovery with marinas and sailing clubs, while managing past legal complexities and preparing for expansion.

I recommended she approach marinas from a customer discovery mindset—asking for advice rather than pitching a product—to gather insights and build relationships. I also suggested connecting with the SBDC for legal and busines

CRITICAL PROBLEM: Reviewing business plan to date and working on financials.They are using LivePlan, so we worked directly on this together.

RECOMMENDATIONS:

  1. Payroll seemed low, and they hadn't put in the two people that are working there now. If they plan to work year round full time, at least one of the employees will have to be laid off midwinter, but this is also a great time for them to take annual vacations and burn up PTO.
  2. Utilities and all expenses were the same each year, figure 5-7% increase per year for utilities and variable expenses. Better to be on the high side than the opposite.
  3. Prior to the meeting, I reviewed the entire plan to date. Wasn't sure where they got the financial figures, but they said it was based on actual from the existing owner in annual costs; he was hesitant to share the actual books until they had an approved offer from the bank.

NEXT STEPS: Jodi will make updates to financials.

﻿B - Natural candle maker for over 15 years, Shannon began selling them for profit about 2 years ago. She sold about $15,000 last year which amazed herself. She said she really hustled to do that and now wants to build a bigger brand online. We talked about lead capture and key components of the biz plan including target market (women in 30s & 40s, into health, beauty, fashion, wellness, yoga. massage, facials.) Problem (traditional candles have (bad) ingredients, irritating. People are living with more stress, anxiety, bad environmental issues, screen-time, overworked, fast-paced society, etc.)

Hey Julie!


So we actually did not get awarded the SBIR funding this last FOA. Though our first submission, we got three out of the four reviewers who provided very positive feedback, The fourth reviewer was definitely an anomaly in terms of their interpretation, though we didn’t let the lack of funding last quarter stop us we’ve actually started developing the software solution as well as speaking with hundreds of industry contacts and thousands of consumer level users. (Everyone uses electronics…). 


We were accepted into Newlab so we have been working between contacts we have made there to further secure funding. 



Something else that I can share is that we recently were accepted into the generator program to further accelerate our solution and support the growth of our community! The official launch of this next cohort is on Friday so we are definitely excited to see where that continues to push us forward!

Hi Evelyn,


It's hard to consider this marketing plan without looking at how it fits into the total financial budget. While I am sure having that large a marketing spend would increase potential leads and revenue, I don't think it is sustainable. Most businesses start with a more basic plan using the limited funds you will have more strategically. Anytime I see spreadsheets with the same recurring dollar amount, I question it. 

Like one or 2 trade shows in a year. Purchasing social media scheduling tools and planning your own posts and website blogs. If you want to plan for some grand opening PR and media, select a budget for that one time expense. I don't think it's wise to borrow money to support an ongoing media campaign. I try to look at spending in a very conservative way.

Let's revisit when you get me the financials. 


Diana

ClientIntake.CompanyName: Explosive Confections of Central Michigan, LLC

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Our client emailed me regarding a tax filing issue. I called her back to talk through what was going on. Pertinent points to the conversation:

  • Our client's husband filed their tax return for 2024 as Married Filing Jointly without including any of her personal income (she receives money on behalf of her father as his designated payee) and her LLC income and expenses.
  • When she brought it to his attention, he asked her to file a separate return for the LLC. An LLC is not like a corporation, which is required to file a return for the separate entity, but is included as a schedule (C, D or F) on the personal tax return.
  • I suggested that he should amend the tax return to either:
    • maintain the current Married Filing Jointly status and include her personal income and LLC income and expenses, or
    • change his status to Married Filing Separately to allow her to file a tax return for her income and expenses as Married Filing Separately

Nicole facilitated webinar on more SEO work meta descriptions, page titles and key words, will then let a month go by and rerun SEO. Client very happy with assistance. Also looked at FB page, suggested maybe an X account be considered, client has done some nice updates on google listing with all 5 star reviews. Hosting events and getting the word out well. Meet again 2 weeks.

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM: He needs general help getting restarted

 

RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN: He has been absent recently to take care of personal business. He has also moved to a new house. He still wants to consider opening a landscape/snow removal business, cleaning business, and a car rental business. He works FT at 2 jobs... cook at Culvers and plant in Holly. He banks with Navy Fed CU and Fin Plus CU. I sent him info on Huntington's LIFT program. He needs to work on fixing his credit score...low 600s, and I noted that 650 is best at a minimum. We discussed using AI to help with making pitches...to 100K, for example.

 

NEXT STEPS & FOLLOW-U
Client has not engaged, I will close for now.
